column1: Gift log - what I made or gave, and for whom
Mostly handmade. The point is the making, not the buying. Kept so I do not repeat myself and do not forget a birthday.

column4: A small pour-over dripper, his coffee
column5: He uses it every morning. Good.

column4: Dinner at Kamo River Teahouse, and a tea cup
column5: Mid May every year. Book the teahouse early.

column4: A small bowl, tenmoku, his favourite
column5: Osaka. He pretends not to be sentimental about them.

column4: Bought the good Yanagi book I keep borrowing
column5: Turned 31. Still reading the same book slowly.

column4: The annual single piece. This year a tea bowl, nuka over iron
column5: The slow gift. One a year since the apprenticeship. The most personal thing I make.

column4: A matched pair of rice bowls, and I cooked Mom's stew
column5: Sunday in Kitayama. Dad said nothing and used his at once.

column4: A jug, the celadon she liked
column5: For covering the studio and the wood rota.

column4: Teahouse again. Make him a new cup, the old one chipped
The annual piece. Decide after the autumn anagama which bowl is good enough
column5: Save three candidates from the wood firing.

column1: The Toshio pieces, kept as a list, are nearly a decade now. One day they should sit together on a shelf. Maybe that is its own small show, one day.
